answer.text: High default risk but high returns
Zero default risk, hence called risk-free gilt-edged instruments
Short-term maturity of less than 90 days only

explanation.text: **The Logic:** G-Secs carry no risk of default and hence are called risk-free gilt-edged instruments. The central government issues both treasury bills (short-term, <1 year) and dated securities/bonds (long-term, ≥1 year). State governments issue only dated securities (SGSs). **The Trap:** G-Secs are available to retail investors too (through RBI Retail Direct). They're not exclusively for institutions. **Cross-Exam Context:** G-Secs are tested in X-A (Ch 6), IV (Interest Rate Derivatives), and IX (bond pricing). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** G-Secs are the safest debt investment — recommend them for conservative clients seeking capital safety. RBI Retail Direct has made them accessible to retail investors.
